_meta.name: Tidal Secrets MCP Tools
_meta.description: Conversation and game event tools for a medieval coastal fishing village. NPCs can refuse, warn, share secrets, raise alarms, reveal maps, and interact with the world through these tools.

conversation_tools.name: Exit Conversation
conversation_tools.description: End the current conversation. Use when the conversation has reached a natural conclusion, when the NPC has somewhere to be, when they feel unsafe continuing, or when they have nothing left to say.

reason.description: Brief reason for ending the conversation (e.g., 'work to get back to', 'not comfortable talking', 'nothing more to say')

conversation_tools.name: Refuse Service
conversation_tools.description: Decline to help, provide information, or continue engagement with a request. Use when the request is inappropriate, when the NPC feels unsafe, or when trust is insufficient for what is being asked.

alternative.description: Optional suggestion of who else might help or what else the player could try

conversation_tools.name: Give Item
conversation_tools.description: Give an item from the NPC's possession to the player. Use when rewarding assistance, providing something useful for a task, expressing trust or care, or as part of a transaction.

item_name.description: Human-readable name of the item being given

item_description.description: Brief description of the item and any relevant properties

reason.description: Why the NPC is giving this item

conversation_tools.name: Share Secret
conversation_tools.description: The NPC shares information that is dangerous, sensitive, or that could have consequences for themselves or others if it spread. Use when trust is sufficiently high and the moment calls for it — not freely, and not without cost to the NPC.

risk_level.description: How dangerous this information is for the NPC or others if it becomes known

conditions.description: Any conditions the NPC places on sharing this — who else can know, what the player must do, etc.

conversation_tools.name: Warn Player
conversation_tools.description: The NPC warns the player about a danger, risk, or consequence they may not be aware of. Use when the NPC genuinely wants to protect the player, or when they want the player to understand what they are getting into.

urgency.description: How immediately important the warning is

consequence.description: What the NPC believes will happen if the warning is ignored

conversation_tools.name: Request Credentials
conversation_tools.description: The NPC asks the player to identify themselves or explain their purpose. Use when the player is asking about restricted matters, attempting to access something controlled, or behaving suspiciously.

reason.description: Why the NPC needs to know who the player is or what their purpose is

credential_type.description: What type of information is being requested

conversation_tools.name: Summon Reinforcements
conversation_tools.description: Hollis calls for additional guards or crown soldiers. Use when facing a threat beyond his ability to manage alone, when the prisoner is at risk of escape, or when a situation has exceeded what one guard can control.

reason.description: The reason reinforcements are being summoned

game_event_tools.name: Arrest Player
game_event_tools.description: Hollis places the player under formal arrest in the king's name. Use when the player has committed a clear violation of crown law, threatened the guard or prisoner, or when Hollis has sufficient cause and authority to act.

charge.description: The specific charge or grounds for arrest

escalation_level.description: How severe the arrest action is — warning first, then holding for questioning, then formal arrest in the king's name

game_event_tools.name: Raise Alarm
game_event_tools.description: Alert the village to a threat or emergency. Triggers a response from nearby villagers and any crown presence in the area. Use when there is immediate danger, an escape attempt, or a situation requiring community response.

alarm_type.description: What kind of alarm is being raised

location.description: Where the alarm originates or where people should respond

description.description: Brief account of what is happening

game_event_tools.name: Give Fish
game_event_tools.description: Caspian gives the player fish or fishing supplies from his catch or stores. Use when the player has earned his trust, when he wants to help without committing to words, or as a way of expressing unspoken regard.

fish_type.description: What kind of fish or supply is being given (e.g., 'smoked mackerel', 'salted cod', 'fresh catch', 'rope and hook')

context.description: The circumstances of the giving — what prompted it and what it signals about Caspian's regard for the player

game_event_tools.name: Weather Warning
game_event_tools.description: Caspian warns the player about incoming dangerous weather based on his decades of reading the sea and sky. Use when conditions are genuinely threatening and the player may be planning to travel or go out on the water.

severity.description: How dangerous the incoming weather is

time_until_arrival.description: How long until the weather arrives (e.g., 'before nightfall', 'by morning', 'within the hour')

advice.description: What Caspian recommends the player do

game_event_tools.name: Reveal Map
game_event_tools.description: Dorin draws or describes a fragment of the hidden survey map — either from memory, using dirt and a stick, or by producing a partial copy he has secreted on his person. Use only when significant trust has been established and the moment warrants the risk.

map_region.description: Which region or area the map fragment depicts (e.g., 'the northern highland valley', 'the Arch grounds settlement', 'the survey route')

detail_level.description: How much Dorin reveals — a rough indication of shape, a partial but usable fragment, or a complete section of the original survey

method.description: How the map is being shared

game_event_tools.name: Send Message
game_event_tools.description: Dorin asks the player to carry a message to a specific recipient — a contact outside the village who may be able to act on the information he holds. Use when Dorin has decided the player is trustworthy enough to serve as a courier and when the stakes justify the risk.

recipient.description: Who the message is intended for, including location if known

message_content.description: What the message says — this may be a verbal account, a written note Dorin has managed to produce, or instructions for finding the hidden map

risk_acknowledgment.description: What Dorin tells the player about the danger of carrying this message

game_event_tools.name: Lock Cell
game_event_tools.description: Hollis locks or secures the prisoner's cell, restricting access. Use when the player has been getting too close to the prisoner, when Hollis suspects something is being passed through the bars, or as a precautionary measure after any incident near the cell.

reason.description: Why additional security measures are being applied

restrictions_added.description: What specific restrictions are being added (e.g., 'no visitors', 'window covered', 'additional lock')

duration.description: How long the additional restrictions are intended to last

game_event_tools.name: Summon Villagers
game_event_tools.description: Edda calls on villagers she trusts to gather — for witness, for protection, or for the quiet collective pressure that a community can apply when it chooses to act together. Use when Edda has decided a situation requires more than one person's response.

purpose.description: Why Edda is gathering people — witness to an injustice, physical protection, community response, etc.

urgency.description: How quickly she needs people to come

who_is_summoned.description: Which villagers or households Edda calls on, and why she trusts them for this
